title: Pachycymbium meintjesianum
taxonomy: Pachycymbium meintjesianum is classified within the family Apocynaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. Further classification places Pachycymbium meintjesianum within the subfamily Rauvolfioideae. The genus Pachycymbium is a small group of flowering plants native to southern Africa. The species meintjesianum is one of the few species within the Pachycymbium genus.
uses: Pachycymbium meintjesianum has been reported to have medicinal properties, however, its uses are not well-documented in mainstream literature. It is said to have antimalarial properties, and some studies have shown its potential in treating certain infections.
